mori

Raises a fatal error or panic.

Aliases: panic

Syntax: mori

Category

errors

Related

  • iace
  • ergo

Examples

examples/corpus/mori/mori.fab (canonical · keyword)

Raises a fatal error or panic.

# mori — fatal unrecoverable abort
#
# mori <nuntius>
#
# GRAMMAR:
#   abortStmt :← 'mori' expr
#
# EXPECTED OUTPUT:
#   Happy-path division and lista access; mori paths are commented out.

functio divide(numerus a, numerus b) → fractus {
    # Use mori for invariant violations
    si b ≡ 0 {
        mori "divisio per nihilum"
    }
    redde (a ↦ fractus) / (b ↦ fractus)
}

functio accipe(lista<numerus> res, numerus index) → numerus {
    # Bounds check with mori
    si index < 0 aut index ≥ res.longitudo() {
        mori "index extra fines"
    }
    redde res[index]
}

incipit {
    # Happy path — mori branches not taken
    fixum _ valor ← divide(10, 2)
    # 5.0
    nota "valor: §"(valor)

    # This would call mori:
    # fixum _ malum ← divide(10, 0)

    # Lista accessus
    fixum _ nums ← [1, 2, 3]
    fixum _ lectum ← accipe(nums, 1)
    # 2
    nota "valor: §"(lectum)
}
Sources
  • ["examples/corpus/mori/mori.fab"]
